# Statistics
> * Statistics is the science of uncertainty & variability
  * Statistics turns data into information
  * Data -> Information -> Knowledge -> Wisdom
  * Data Driven Decisions (3Ds)
  * Statistics is the interpretation of Science
  * Statistics is the Art & Science of learning from data

 

# Variable
> * Characteristic that may vary from individual to individual
  * Height, Weight, CGPA etc


 

# Measurement
> * Process of assigning numbers or labels to objects or states in accordance with logically accepted rules

 

# Measurement Scales
> * **Nominal Scale:** Obersvations may be classified into mutually exclusive & exhaustive classes or categories
* **Ordinal Scale:** Obersvations may be ranked
* **Interval Scale:** Difference between obersvations is meaningful
* **Ratio Scale:** Ratio between obersvations is meaningful & true zero point

 

## Nominal Data

### Example
> The following data shows the gender of a sample of twenty students from the University of Agriculture, Faisalabad.

 

  Student  | Gender
  ---------|--------
     	1    |  Male
     	2    |  Male
     	3    |  Female
     	4    |  Female
     	5    |  Female
     	6    |  Female
     	7    |  Male
     	8    |  Male
     	9    |  Male
     10    |  Male
     11    |  Female
     12    |  Female
     13    |  Male
     14    |  Male
     15    |  Female
     16    |  Female
     17    |  Male
     18    |  Male
     19    |  Male
     20    |  Male

 


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
if (!require("tidyverse")) install.packages("tidyverse")
# library(tidyverse)
df1 <- tibble::tibble(
    Student = seq(from = 1, to = 20, by = 1)
  , Gender  = rep(x = c("Male", "Female", "Male", "Female", "Male", "Female", "Male"), c(2, 4, 4, 2, 2, 2, 4))
  )
df1
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 20 × 2
   Student Gender
     <dbl> <chr> 
 1       1 Male  
 2       2 Male  
 3       3 Female
 4       4 Female
 5       5 Female
 6       6 Female
 7       7 Male  
 8       8 Male  
 9       9 Male  
10      10 Male  
11      11 Female
12      12 Female
13      13 Male  
14      14 Male  
15      15 Female
16      16 Female
17      17 Male  
18      18 Male  
19      19 Male  
20      20 Male  
```


:::
:::


#### Frequency Table


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df1Freq <- 
      df1 %>%
      dplyr::count(Gender) %>%
      dplyr::rename(f = n) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        rf = f/sum(f)
      , pf = rf*100  
      )
df1Freq
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 2 × 4
  Gender     f    rf    pf
  <chr>  <int> <dbl> <dbl>
1 Female     8   0.4    40
2 Male      12   0.6    60
```


:::
:::


#### Simple Bar Chart


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df1
    ,  mapping = aes(x = Gender)) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Simple Bar Chart", x = "Gender", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



 

### Example
> Following data presents the number of nucleotides of gene sequence (A, C, G, T). This is illustrated by the Zyxin gene which plays an important role in cell adhesion (Golub et al, 1999). The accession number (X94991.1) of one of its variants can be found in a data base like NCBI (UniGene). Given data will be used to illustrate the construction of pie chart from the frequency table of four nucleotides.

   A   |  C  | G   |T
 ------|-----|-----|------
  410  | 789 | 573 | 394 


> Data from the GenBank can also be imported directly by the following code.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
# install.packages(pkgs = "ape", repo = "http://cran.r-project.org", dependencies =TRUE)
# library(ape)
# df2 <- table(read.GenBank(c("X94991.1"),as.character=TRUE))
# df2
```
:::


#### Frequency Table

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df3 <- tibble(
    G.Sequence   = c("A", "C", "G", "T")
  , Nucleotides  = c(410, 789, 573, 394)
  )
df3
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 4 × 2
  G.Sequence Nucleotides
  <chr>            <dbl>
1 A                  410
2 C                  789
3 G                  573
4 T                  394
```


:::
:::


#### Simple Bar Chart

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
        data = df3
      , mapping = aes(x = G.Sequence, y = Nucleotides)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Simple Bar Chart", x = "Gene Sequence", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::


## Ordinal Data

### Example
> The following data shows the grades of a sample of twenty students from the University of Agriculture, Faisalabad.

 

  Student  | Grade
  ---------|--------
     	1    |  A
     	2    |  B
     	3    |  B
     	4    |  C
     	5    |  A
     	6    |  D
     	7    |  F
     	8    |  C
     	9    |  B
     10    |  D
     11    |  F
     12    |  A
     13    |  B
     14    |  B
     15    |  C
     16    |  D
     17    |  C
     18    |  B
     19    |  C
     20    |  D

 


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df4 <- tibble::tibble(
    Student = seq(from = 1, to = 20, by = 1)
  , Grade  = c("A", "B", "B", "C", "A", "D", "F", "C", "B", "D", "F", "A", "B", "B", "C", "D", "C",  "B", "C", "D")
  )
df4
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 20 × 2
   Student Grade
     <dbl> <chr>
 1       1 A    
 2       2 B    
 3       3 B    
 4       4 C    
 5       5 A    
 6       6 D    
 7       7 F    
 8       8 C    
 9       9 B    
10      10 D    
11      11 F    
12      12 A    
13      13 B    
14      14 B    
15      15 C    
16      16 D    
17      17 C    
18      18 B    
19      19 C    
20      20 D    
```


:::
:::




#### Frequency Table


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df4Freq <- 
      df4 %>%
      dplyr::count(Grade) %>%
      dplyr::rename(f = n) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        rf = f/sum(f)
      , pf = rf*100
      , cf = cumsum(f)
      )
df4Freq
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 5
  Grade     f    rf    pf    cf
  <chr> <int> <dbl> <dbl> <int>
1 A         3  0.15    15     3
2 B         6  0.3     30     9
3 C         5  0.25    25    14
4 D         4  0.2     20    18
5 F         2  0.1     10    20
```


:::
:::


#### Simple Bar Chart


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df4
    ,  mapping = aes(x = Grade)) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Simple Bar Chart", x = "Grades", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



 

## Two Way Contingency Table
### Example
> The following data shows the gender and residental status of a sample of twenty students from the University of Agriculture, Faisalabad.

 

  Student  | Gender  | RS
  ---------|---------|--------------
     	1    |  Male   | Boarding
     	2    |  Male   | Non-Boarding
     	3    |  Female | Non-Boarding
     	4    |  Female | Boarding
     	5    |  Female | Boarding
     	6    |  Female | Non-Boarding
     	7    |  Male   | Non-Boarding
     	8    |  Male   | Boarding
     	9    |  Male   | Non-Boarding
     10    |  Male   | Non-Boarding
     11    |  Female | Non-Boarding
     12    |  Female | Boarding
     13    |  Male   | Non-Boarding
     14    |  Male   | Non-Boarding
     15    |  Female | Non-Boarding
     16    |  Female | Non-Boarding  
     17    |  Male   | Non-Boarding
     18    |  Male   | Non-Boarding
     19    |  Male   | Non-Boarding
     20    |  Male   | Boarding

 


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df5 <- tibble::tibble(
    Student = seq(from = 1, to = 20, by = 1)
  , Gender  = rep(x = c("Male", "Female", "Male", "Female", "Male", "Female", "Male"), c(2, 4, 4, 2, 2, 2, 4))
  , RS      = c("B", "NB", "NB", "B", "B", "NB", "NB", "B", "NB", "NB", "NB", "B", "NB", "NB", "NB", "NB", "NB", "NB", "NB", "B")
  )
df5
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 20 × 3
   Student Gender RS   
     <dbl> <chr>  <chr>
 1       1 Male   B    
 2       2 Male   NB   
 3       3 Female NB   
 4       4 Female B    
 5       5 Female B    
 6       6 Female NB   
 7       7 Male   NB   
 8       8 Male   B    
 9       9 Male   NB   
10      10 Male   NB   
11      11 Female NB   
12      12 Female B    
13      13 Male   NB   
14      14 Male   NB   
15      15 Female NB   
16      16 Female NB   
17      17 Male   NB   
18      18 Male   NB   
19      19 Male   NB   
20      20 Male   B    
```


:::
:::


#### Cross Tables


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df5 %>%
  dplyr::count(Gender) %>%
  dplyr::rename(f = n)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 2 × 2
  Gender     f
  <chr>  <int>
1 Female     8
2 Male      12
```


:::
:::




::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df5 %>%
  dplyr::count(RS) %>%
  dplyr::rename(f = n)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 2 × 2
  RS        f
  <chr> <int>
1 B         6
2 NB       14
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df5 %>%
  dplyr::count(Gender, RS) %>%
  dplyr::rename(f = n)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 4 × 3
  Gender RS        f
  <chr>  <chr> <int>
1 Female B         3
2 Female NB        5
3 Male   B         3
4 Male   NB        9
```


:::
:::




::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
if (!require("janitor")) install.packages("janitor")
# library(janitor)
df5CrossTab <- 
      df5 %>%
      janitor::tabyl(dat = ., var1 = Gender, var2 = RS) %>%
      janitor::adorn_totals(dat = ., where = c("row", "col"))
df5CrossTab
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
 Gender B NB Total
 Female 3  5     8
   Male 3  9    12
  Total 6 14    20
```


:::
:::


#### Simple Bar Charts

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df5
    ,  mapping = aes(x = Gender)) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Simple Bar Chart", x = "Gender", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-15-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::




::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df5
    ,  mapping = aes(x = RS)) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Simple Bar Chart", x = "Residental Status", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-16-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::


#### Multiple Bar Charts

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df5
    ,  mapping = aes(x = Gender, fill = RS)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Multiple Bar Chart", x = "Gender", fill = "Residental Status", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-17-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df5
    ,  mapping = aes(x = RS, fill = Gender)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Multiple Bar Chart", x = "Residental Status", fill = "Gender", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-18-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



#### Component Bar Charts

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df5
    ,  mapping = aes(x = Gender, fill = RS)) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Component Bar Chart", x = "Gender", fill = "Residental Status", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-19-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df5
    ,  mapping = aes(x = RS, fill = Gender)) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Component Bar Chart", x = "Residental Status", fill = "Gender", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-20-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::


### Example
> Source: OMB Statistical Policy Working Paper 22. https://www.hhs.gov/sites/default/files/spwp22.pdf
Following data set consists of information concerning delinquent children. Recorded variables are Number of Delinquent Children by County and Education Level of Household Head.

#### Cross Tables

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df6 <- tibble(
  Delinquent = gl(n = 4, k = 4, length = 16, labels = c("Alpha", "Beta", "Gamma", "Delta"))
, EduLevel   = gl(n = 4, k = 1, length = 16, labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High", "Very High"))
, Freq       = c(15, 0, 5, 0, 20, 10, 10, 15, 5, 10, 10, 0, 10, 15, 5, 5)
)

df6
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 16 × 3
   Delinquent EduLevel   Freq
   <fct>      <fct>     <dbl>
 1 Alpha      Low          15
 2 Alpha      Medium        0
 3 Alpha      High          5
 4 Alpha      Very High     0
 5 Beta       Low          20
 6 Beta       Medium       10
 7 Beta       High         10
 8 Beta       Very High    15
 9 Gamma      Low           5
10 Gamma      Medium       10
11 Gamma      High         10
12 Gamma      Very High     0
13 Delta      Low          10
14 Delta      Medium       15
15 Delta      High          5
16 Delta      Very High     5
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df6 %>%
  xtabs(data = ., Freq ~ Delinquent)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Delinquent
Alpha  Beta Gamma Delta 
   20    55    25    35 
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df6 %>%
  xtabs(data = ., Freq ~ EduLevel)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
EduLevel
      Low    Medium      High Very High 
       50        35        30        20 
```


:::
:::




::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df6 %>%
  xtabs(data = ., Freq ~ Delinquent + EduLevel)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
          EduLevel
Delinquent Low Medium High Very High
     Alpha  15      0    5         0
     Beta   20     10   10        15
     Gamma   5     10   10         0
     Delta  10     15    5         5
```


:::
:::


#### Multiple Bar Charts

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df6
    ,  mapping = aes(x = Delinquent, y = Freq, fill = EduLevel)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Multiple Bar Chart", x = "Delinquent", fill = "Education Level", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-25-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df6
    ,  mapping = aes(x = EduLevel, y = Freq, fill = Delinquent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Multiple Bar Chart", x = "Education Level", fill = "Delinquent", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-26-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



#### Component Bar Charts

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}

       data = df6
    ,  mapping = aes(x = Delinquent, y = Freq, fill = EduLevel)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Component Bar Chart", x = "Delinquent", fill = "Education Level", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-27-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df6
    ,  mapping = aes(x = EduLevel, y = Freq, fill = Delinquent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Component Bar Chart", x = "Education Level", fill = "Delinquent", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-28-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}

## Count Data
### Example
> The following data shows the number of notebook a sample of twenty students keeping.

 

  Student  | Notebook
  ---------|---------
     	1    |  3
     	2    |  1
     	3    |  0
     	4    |  2
     	5    |  2
     	6    |  4
     	7    |  5
     	8    |  1
     	9    |  1
     10    |  2
     11    |  3
     12    |  4
     13    |  2
     14    |  5
     15    |  1
     16    |  5
     17    |  4
     18    |  2
     19    |  2
     20    |  3

 


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df7 <- tibble::tibble(
    Student  = seq(from = 1, to = 20, by = 1)
  , Notebook = c(3, 1, 0, 2, 2, 4, 5, 1, 1, 2, 3, 4, 2, 5, 1, 5, 4, 2, 2, 3)
  )
df7
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 20 × 2
   Student Notebook
     <dbl>    <dbl>
 1       1        3
 2       2        1
 3       3        0
 4       4        2
 5       5        2
 6       6        4
 7       7        5
 8       8        1
 9       9        1
10      10        2
11      11        3
12      12        4
13      13        2
14      14        5
15      15        1
16      16        5
17      17        4
18      18        2
19      19        2
20      20        3
```


:::
:::




#### Frequency Table


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df7Freq <- 
      df7 %>%
      dplyr::count(Notebook) %>%
      dplyr::rename(f = n) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        rf = f/sum(f)
      , pf = rf*100
      , cf = cumsum(f)
      )
df7Freq
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 6 × 5
  Notebook     f    rf    pf    cf
     <dbl> <int> <dbl> <dbl> <int>
1        0     1  0.05     5     1
2        1     4  0.2     20     5
3        2     6  0.3     30    11
4        3     3  0.15    15    14
5        4     3  0.15    15    17
6        5     3  0.15    15    20
```


:::
:::


#### Simple Bar Chart


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df7
    ,  mapping = aes(x = Notebook)) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Simple Bar Chart", x = "Notebooks", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-31-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



 

## Continuous Data
### Example
> The following data is the final plant height (cm) of thirty plants of wheat. 
87	91	89	88	89	91	87	92	90	98	95	97	96	100	101
96	98	99	98	100	102	99	101	105	103	107	105	106	107	112


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df8 <- tibble::tibble(
  PlantHeight = c(
  87,	91,	89,	88,	89,	91,	87,	92,	90,	98,	95,	97,	96,	100,	101,
  96,	98,	99,	98,	100,	102,	99,	101,	105,	103,	107,	105,	106,	107,	112
  )
)
df8
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 30 × 1
   PlantHeight
         <dbl>
 1          87
 2          91
 3          89
 4          88
 5          89
 6          91
 7          87
 8          92
 9          90
10          98
# ℹ 20 more rows
```


:::
:::


#### Frequency Distribution


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df9 <- df8 %>% 
  summarize(
    R = max(PlantHeight) - min(PlantHeight)
  , k = floor(1 + 3.3*log10(length(PlantHeight)))
  , h = R/k
    )

df8Freq <- df8 %>% 
  mutate(
          Classes = cut(
                         x              = PlantHeight
                       , breaks         = df9$k
                       , include.lowest = TRUE
                       , right          = FALSE
                       )
          ) %>%
  count(Classes) %>% 
  tidyr::separate(col = Classes, into = c("LB", "UB"), sep = ",", remove = FALSE) %>%
  rename(f = n) %>%
  mutate(
    LB = readr::parse_number(x = LB)
  , UB = readr::parse_number(x = UB)
  , rf = f/sum(f)
  , pf = f/sum(f)*100
  , cf = cumsum(f)
  , MidPoint = (LB + UB)/2
  ) 
  
df8Freq
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 8
  Classes      LB    UB     f    rf    pf    cf MidPoint
  <fct>     <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl> <dbl> <int>    <dbl>
1 [87,92)      87    92     8 0.267  26.7     8     89.5
2 [92,97)      92    97     4 0.133  13.3    12     94.5
3 [97,102)     97   102    10 0.333  33.3    22     99.5
4 [102,107)   102   107     5 0.167  16.7    27    104. 
5 [107,112]   107   112     3 0.1    10      30    110. 
```


:::
:::


#### Histogram


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(
       data = df8
     , mapping = aes(x = PlantHeight)) + 
  geom_histogram() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw()+
    labs(title = "Histogram for Plant Height", x = "Plant Height", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-34-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::





::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data = df8Freq, mapping = aes(x = MidPoint, y = f))  + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_line() + 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw()+
    labs(title = "Frequency for Plant Height", x = "Mid Point", y = "Frequency") + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-35-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data = df8Freq, mapping = aes(x = MidPoint, y = cf)) + 
  geom_point()+ 
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw()+
    labs(title = "Cummulative Frequency Polygon", x = "Mid Point", y = "Cummulative Frequency") + 
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](2018-09-20_BioInfoR_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-36-1.svg){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::

### Example
> The golub table contains gene expression values from 3051 genes taken from 38 Leukemia patients. Twenty seven patients are diagnosed as acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) and eleven as acute myeloid leukemia (AML). The golub.gnames table contains information on the gene, including gene index, manufacturing ID, and biological name. Following table presents the gene expression value by their tumor type.

tumortype|genevalue|tumortype|genevalue
-------- |-------- |--------- |--------
ALL | 2.10892 | ALL  | 1.78352 
ALL | 1.52405 | ALL  | 0.45827 
ALL | 1.96403 | ALL  | 2.18119 
ALL | 2.33597 | ALL  | 2.31428 
ALL | 1.85111 | ALL  | 1.99927 
ALL | 1.99391 | ALL  | 1.36844 
ALL | 2.06597 | ALL  | 2.37351 
ALL | 1.81649 | ALL  | 1.83485 
ALL | 2.17622 | AML  | 0.88941 
ALL | 1.80861 | AML  | 1.45014 
ALL | 2.44562 | AML  | 0.42904 
ALL | 1.90496 | AML  | 0.82667 
ALL | 2.76610 | AML  | 0.63637 
ALL | 1.32551 | AML  | 1.02250
ALL | 2.59385 | AML  | 0.12758 
ALL | 1.92776 | AML  | -0.74333 
ALL | 1.10546 | AML  | 0.73784 
ALL | 1.27645 | AML  | 0.49470 
ALL | 1.83051 | AML  | 1.12058


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df10 <- tibble(
   genevalue = c(
              2.10892, 1.52405, 1.96403, 2.33597, 1.85111, 1.99391
            , 2.06597, 1.81649, 2.17622, 1.80861, 2.44562, 1.90496
            , 2.76610, 1.32551, 2.59385, 1.92776, 1.10546, 1.27645
            , 1.83051, 1.78352, 0.45827, 2.18119, 2.31428, 1.99927
            , 1.36844, 2.37351, 1.83485, 0.88941, 1.45014, 0.42904
            , 0.82667, 0.63637, 1.02250, 0.12758, -0.74333, 0.73784
            , 0.49470, 1.12058
            )
  , tumortype = rep(c("ALL","AML"), c(27, 11))
)

df10
```

#### Frequency Distribution


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df11 <- df10 %>% 
  summarize(
    R = max(genevalue) - min(genevalue)
  , k = floor(1 + 3.3*log10(length(genevalue)))
  , h = R/k
    )

df10Freq <- df10 %>% 
  mutate(
          Classes = cut(
                         x              = genevalue
                       , breaks         = df11$k
                       , include.lowest = TRUE
                       , right          = FALSE
                       )
          ) %>%
  count(Classes) %>% 
  tidyr::separate(col = Classes, into = c("LB", "UB"), sep = ",", remove = FALSE) %>%
  rename(f = n) %>%
  mutate(
    LB = readr::parse_number(x = LB)
  , UB = readr::parse_number(x = UB)
  , rf = f/sum(f)
  , pf = f/sum(f)*100
  , cf = cumsum(f)
  , MidPoint = (LB + UB)/2
  ) 
  
df10Freq
```

# Measures of Centeral Tendency


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df7 %>%
  summarize(
            n       = length(Notebook)
          , Mean    = mean(Notebook)
          , Median  = median(Notebook)
          , Minimum = min(Notebook) 
          , Maximum = max(Notebook) 
          , Q1      = quantile(x = Notebook, probs = 0.25)
          , Q2      = quantile(x = Notebook, probs = 0.50)
          , Q3      = quantile(x = Notebook, probs = 0.75)
  )
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 8
      n  Mean Median Minimum Maximum    Q1    Q2    Q3
  <int> <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
1    20   2.6      2       0       5  1.75     2     4
```


:::
:::




::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df8 %>%
  summarize(
            n       = length(PlantHeight)
          , Mean    = mean(PlantHeight)
          , Median  = median(PlantHeight)
          , Minimum = min(PlantHeight) 
          , Maximum = max(PlantHeight) 
          , Q1      = quantile(x = PlantHeight, probs = 0.25)
          , Q2      = quantile(x = PlantHeight, probs = 0.50)
          , Q3      = quantile(x = PlantHeight, probs = 0.75)
  )
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 8
      n  Mean Median Minimum Maximum    Q1    Q2    Q3
  <int> <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
1    30  97.6     98      87     112  91.2    98  102.
```


:::
:::




::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df10 %>%
  group_by(tumortype) %>%
  summarize(
            n       = length(genevalue)
          , Mean    = mean(genevalue)
          , Median  = median(genevalue)
          , Minimum = min(genevalue) 
          , Maximum = max(genevalue) 
          , Q1      = quantile(x = genevalue, probs = 0.25)
          , Q2      = quantile(x = genevalue, probs = 0.50)
          , Q3      = quantile(x = genevalue, probs = 0.75)
  )
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 2 × 9
  tumortype     n  Mean Median Minimum Maximum    Q1    Q2    Q3
  <chr>     <int> <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
1 ALL          27 1.89   1.93    0.458    2.77 1.80  1.93  2.18
```

# Measures of Dispersion


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df7 %>%
  summarize(
            IQR      = IQR(Notebook)
          , Variance = var(Notebook)
          ,  SD      = sd(Notebook)
          )
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 3
    IQR Variance    SD
  <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>
1  2.25     2.25  1.50
```


:::
:::





::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df8 %>%
  summarize(
            IQR      = IQR(PlantHeight)
          , Variance = var(PlantHeight)
          ,  SD      = sd(PlantHeight)
          )
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 3
    IQR Variance    SD
  <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>
1  10.5     45.0  6.71
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
df10 %>%
  group_by(tumortype) %>%
  summarize(
            IQR      = IQR(genevalue)
          , Variance = var(genevalue)
          ,  SD      = sd(genevalue)
          )
```

```

2 AML       0.494    0.338 0.582
```

# Measures of Skewness


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}

  summarize(
           SK = sum((Notebook - mean(Notebook))^3)/(n()*(sd(Notebook))^3)
          )
```
